title: Starbucks is paying baristas to make TikToks. Expect more brands to follow.
tldr: Starbucks launched an employee-generated ad program paying baristas to create TikToks, putting real employees at the center of brand content. A signal that EGC is going mainstream as a paid, structured program.
angle: Why Starbucks paying baristas signals EGC's shift from grassroots to governed program — and what governance guardrails brands need.

title: Sociable: Reddit launches 'People are the Best' campaign
tldr: Reddit's new campaign celebrates real human conversations on-platform while taking subtle aim at AI-generated content. A clear positioning play around humanity over synthetic polish.
angle: How 'people over polish' is becoming a competitive brand differentiator in the AI content era.

title: UK media consumption trends in 2026: Are smartphones impacting attention spans in the UK?
tldr: YouGov data digs into how UK audiences consume media in 2026 and whether smartphones are eroding attention. Direct evidence for anyone rethinking content formats around real consumption habits.
angle: What shrinking attention spans mean for content architecture — designing for fragmented, mobile-first consumption.

title: How John Deere steeps its brand story in its American heritage
tldr: A breakdown of how John Deere anchors its brand narrative in American heritage — a concrete corporate storytelling case study.
why_fresh: solid but evergreen brand story case study
angle: How legacy brands mine heritage for narrative — and when nostalgia becomes a trap.

title: Future of Marketing Briefing: Why Bose is building an entertainment company
tldr: Bose launched an entertainment division and its CMO hasn't used a creative agency in five years — a case study in brands becoming owned-media publishers.
angle: The death of the creative agency? What brands building in-house entertainment arms means for content ops.

title: Creators and AI took center stage at Cannes Lions 2026
tldr: eMarketer's recap of Cannes 2026 shows creators and AI dominated the agenda — a useful snapshot of where the industry's attention and money is heading.
angle: What Cannes 2026's creator-and-AI obsession reveals about the next 12 months of content strategy.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Top-ranked pages may not get cited in Google's AI Overviews, breaking the old SEO logic. Content strategists need to rethink what 'visibility' means now.
angle: Why ranking #1 no longer means being seen — designing content to be cited by AI, not just crawled.

title: Inside the PR response to the Reflecting Pool algae controversy
tldr: A behind-the-scenes look at how a PR team handled the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool controversy by leading with science, facts, and visible proof. A useful crisis-comms case study.
angle: When facts and visible proof beat spin — a crisis playbook for politically charged flashpoints.

title: The 4 PR and Marketing Metrics that Matter for AI Visibility
tldr: Gini Dietrich argues impressions, reach and AVEs won't survive scrutiny, laying out four metrics that matter for AI visibility in 2026. Practical measurement thinking for the AI-search era.
why_fresh: new measurement framework for AI visibility
angle: Retiring vanity metrics: what to actually measure when AI engines mediate your audience.

title: Semrush Releases Expanded 2026 AI Visibility Index, Analyzing 126 Million AI Search Prompts
tldr: Semrush analyzed 126M AI search prompts to map which brands surface in AI answers. A large-scale dataset on the emerging AI-visibility battleground.
why_fresh: large new dataset on AI search
angle: What 126M AI prompts reveal about which content gets surfaced — and how to earn a citation.

title: Bobcat's CMO says AI search is rewriting the customer journey
tldr: Bobcat's CMO details how LLM-based search is reshaping the customer journey and forcing a content-strategy rethink. A B2B-grounded view on AI search's real impact.
angle: How AI search is quietly rewiring the B2B buyer journey — and what content teams must build.

title: Rachel Many's posters highlight how creative mothers are paying twice for an industry in freefall
tldr: A designer turns the economics of motherhood in creative work into sharp visual data-storytelling backed by hard numbers. A strong example of information design meeting advocacy.
angle: Turning uncomfortable statistics into visual arguments — data storytelling as advocacy.

title: Andrew Tindall: What Ritson and Sharp got right (and wrong) at Cannes
tldr: A sharp critique of the Ritson/Sharp marketing-effectiveness debate at Cannes, weighing what the industry's dominant theories get right and wrong. Fuel for a contrarian POV.
why_fresh: fresh debate recap on marketing effectiveness
angle: Where the reigning marketing-effectiveness orthodoxy breaks down for content and comms teams.

title: How TikTok Shop is changing innovation for food and beverage brands
tldr: PepsiCo and Mars are using TikTok Shop not just to sell but to inform new-product development. A concrete look at commerce data feeding brand innovation.
angle: When your storefront becomes your R&D lab: social commerce as an insight engine.
